diagrams.title: Wait-For Graph with Deadlock Cycle
diagrams.description: Shows four processes P1, P2, P3, P4 with wait-for edges forming a cycle
elements: P1→P2 (P1 waits for resource held by P2)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how cycles in WFG represent deadlocks in distributed systems

diagrams.title: Resource Allocation Graph Example
diagrams.description: RAG showing resource instances R1, R2 with allocation and request edges
elements: Resource rectangles R1 (2 instances), R2 (1 instance)
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing resource allocation and deadlock conditions

diagrams.title: Chandy-Misra-Haas Probe Flow
diagrams.description: Step-by-step probe message propagation to detect deadlock
elements: Step 2: Probe sent along P1→P2 edge (P1, P1, P2)
Step 3: Probe to P3 (P1, P2, P3)
Step 4: Probe returns to P1 - DEADLOCK DETECTED
diagrams.purpose: Understanding edge chasing algorithm in distributed deadlock detection

diagrams.title: Centralized vs Hierarchical vs Distributed Control
diagrams.description: Three organization structures for deadlock detection
elements: Centralized: Single coordinator with all sites reporting to it
Hierarchical: Tree structure with root and leaf coordinators
Distributed: Mesh topology with equal peer-to-peer communication
diagrams.purpose: Comparing different control organizations for distributed deadlock handling
